data.narrative:
Overland AI is a Series A-stage Defense & Security company founded by Byron Boots, Greg Okopal, and Stephanie Bonk, having raised $40M+. The peer universe comprises 10 private companies and 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS), with primary valuation anchors drawn from three same-sector, same-stage peers: Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-Progression uses the $40M total raised as a proxy base and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical for Series A advancement, yielding $80M. Peer-Median derives a $300M valuation from the three-company sample, with an observed range of $150M to $500M. Both methods are applicable given the availability of direct sector and stage comparables.

Triangulation across these two approaches supports a valuation range of $68M (low) to $345M (high), with a median of $190M. The wide dispersion—spanning $277M—reflects tension between Overland's pre-deployment funding profile and the substantially higher valuations of established defense-tech peers at the same stage. Key uncertainties include the revenue or deployment status of comparables relative to Overland, customer concentration or contract visibility, and whether the peer set adequately reflects sector-specific technology or defensibility differences. Additional diligence on customer traction would narrow this range materially.
